message:
fix(cli): correct --bundle-version flag name in bundle command tests

Update the unit test for bundle command argument parsing to use the
correct flag name `--bundle-version` instead of `--version`.

The `--version` flag is typically reserved for displaying the program
version (a clap convention), while `--bundle-version` is the actual
flag for specifying the semantic version of the bundle being created.

This test was likely passing before due to clap's prefix matching, but
using the exact flag name makes the test more accurate and prevents
potential ambiguity issues.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
